_usage_note: This template is for REFERENCE ONLY. The actual init command (cmd/init_cmd.go) constructs colony state programmatically. Do NOT use this template for programmatic state creation or file-copy initialization. The Go code is the single source of truth for state structure.
_instructions: This file shows the shape of COLONY_STATE.json with example values. All underscore-prefixed keys (_template, _version, _usage_note, _instructions, and all _comment_*) are metadata and should be removed before writing the final file.
_comment_goal: The user's goal string exactly as provided to /ant-init.
_comment_colony_name: Set during first charter-write. Human-readable name derived from repo directory or package.json.
_comment_colony_version: Integer counter incremented each time the colony is sealed. Starts at 1 on colony initialization. Reset to 0 on entomb (ready for next colony). Tracks how many times this colony has been sealed.
_comment_session: Session ID format: session_{unix_timestamp}_{random}. Timestamps use ISO-8601 UTC format. The same timestamp value is used in both 'initialized_at' and the events array entry.
_comment_memory: phase_learnings and instincts are JSON arrays. Empty [] if no prior colony found, or [{...}, ...] if inherited from completion-report.md. IMPORTANT: both must be JSON arrays, not strings.
_comment_events: Each entry is a pipe-delimited string: 'ISO8601_TIMESTAMP|event_type|source|description'. The initialization event uses the same timestamp as 'initialized_at'. Only the single initialization entry is present at init time.
_comment_parallel_mode: Parallel execution strategy for colony work. Set during /ant-init based on user choice. Valid values: 'in-repo' (default, all workers share the repo checkout) or 'worktree' (each worker gets an isolated git worktree). Omitted or empty means 'in-repo' will be used at runtime.
goal: Build a REST API for task management
events: 2026-01-15T10:30:00Z|colony_initialized|init|Colony initialized with goal: Build a REST API for task management
